intro S T h

      <;>

      <;>

  exact h₂

intro S T h
  have h₁ : (S : χ.Charges) = (T : χ.Charges) := by
    calc
      (S : χ.Charges) = χ.quadSolsIncl S := by
        -- Since `χ.quadSolsIncl S = S.val`, we have `S.val = S`
        simp [χ.quadSolsIncl, χ.quadSolsInclLinSols, χ.linSolsIncl]
        <;>
        (try cases S <;> simp_all [QuadSols.val, LinSols.val]) <;>
        (try rfl)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
        (try aesop)
      _ = χ.quadSolsIncl T := h
      _ = (T : χ.Charges) := by
        -- Similarly, `χ.quadSolsIncl T = T.val`
        simp [χ.quadSolsIncl, χ.quadSolsInclLinSols, χ.linSolsIncl]
        <;>
        (try cases T <;> simp_all [QuadSols.val, LinSols.val]) <;>
        (try rfl)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
        (try aesop)
  -- Now we have `S.val = T.val`, which means `S.toLinSols.val = T.toLinSols.val`
  -- By the injectivity of `χ.toACCSystemLinear.linSolsIncl_injective`, we get `S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols`
  -- And then by `QuadSols.ext`, we get `S = T`.
  have h₂ : S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols := by
    have h₃ : (S.toLinSols : χ.LinSols).val = (T.toLinSols : χ.LinSols).val := by
      simp_all [QuadSols.val]
    have h₄ : Function.Injective (χ.toACCSystemLinear.linSolsIncl : χ.toACCSystemLinear.LinSols → χ.toACCSystemLinear.Charges) := by
      apply χ.toACCSystemLinear.linSolsIncl_injective
    have h₅ : (S.toLinSols : χ.LinSols) = (T.toLinSols : χ.LinSols) := by
      apply h₄
      simp_all [χ.toACCSystemLinear.linSolsIncl]
      <;>
      (try cases S <;> cases T <;> simp_all [QuadSols.val, LinSols.val]) <;>
      (try rfl) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try aesop)
    exact h₅
  -- Now that we have `S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols`, we can deduce `S = T`
  cases S <;> cases T <;> simp_all [QuadSols.ext_iff]
  <;>
  (try contradiction) <;>
  (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
  (try aesop)

-- Prove that the function is injective by showing that if two elements are mapped to the same value, they must be equal.
  have h1 : Function.Injective χ.linSolsIncl := by
    intro x y h
    apply x.ext
    simpa using h
  have h2 : Function.Injective (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols → χ.LinSols) := by
    intro x y h
    cases x
    cases y
    simp_all [QuadSols.ext_iff]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [Function.Injective]) <;>
    (try aesop)
  -- Use the injectivity of the individual components to prove the injectivity of the composition.
  intro x y h
  have h3 : χ.quadSolsIncl x = χ.quadSolsIncl y := h
  have h4 : (χ.quadSolsIncl : χ.QuadSols → χ.1.Charges) = (χ.linSolsIncl : χ.LinSols → χ.1.Charges) ∘ (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols → χ.LinSols) := by
    funext x
    simp [mulActionHom_toLinSolsInclLinSols, mulActionHom_toLinSolsInclCharges]
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Pi.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply]) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try ext i; simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
    (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
    (try omega)
  rw [h4] at h3
  have h5 : (χ.linSolsIncl : χ.LinSols → χ.1.Charges) ((χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols → χ.LinSols) x) = (χ.linSolsIncl : χ.LinSols → χ.1.Charges) ((χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols → χ.LinSols) y) := h3
  have h6 : (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols → χ.LinSols) x = (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols → χ.LinSols) y := by
    apply h1
    exact h5
  have h7 : x = y := by
    apply h2
    exact h6
  exact h7

intro A B h
  have h₁ : (χ.quadSolsIncl A : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) := h
  have h₂ : (A : χ.Charges) = (B : χ.Charges) := by
    have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl A : χ.Charges) = A.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    have h₄ : (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) = B.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    rw [h₃] at h₁
    rw [h₄] at h₁
    exact h₁
  -- Since A.val = B.val, we can use the injectivity of the inclusion from QuadSols to Charges
  have h₃ : A.toLinSols.val = B.toLinSols.val := by
    simp_all [QuadSols.toLinSols]
  have h₄ : A.toLinSols = B.toLinSols := by
    apply injective_iff_leftInverse.mpr (linSolsIncl_injective χ).1
    <;> simp_all [linSolsIncl]
  -- Now we need to show that A = B using the fact that A.toLinSols = B.toLinSols
  have h₅ : A = B := by
    have h₆ : A.toLinSols = B.toLinSols := h₄
    have h₇ : A.val = B.val := by
      simp_all [QuadSols.toLinSols]
    -- Since A.val = B.val and the qadratic conditions are preserved, A = B
    cases A <;> cases B <;> simp_all [QuadSols.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  exact h₅

lemma quadSolsIncl_injective (χ : ACCSystemQuad) :
    Function.Injective χ.quadSolsIncl := by
  intro A B h
  have h₁ : (χ.quadSolsIncl A : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) := h
  have h₂ : (A : χ.Charges) = (B : χ.Charges) := by
    have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl A : χ.Charges) = A.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    have h₄ : (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) = B.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    rw [h₃] at h₁
    rw [h₄] at h₁
    exact h₁
  have h₃ : A = B := by
    -- Since A.val = B.val, we can use the injectivity of the inclusion from QuadSols to Charges
    have h₄ : A.val = B.val := h₂
    -- Use the fact that two QuadSols are equal if their values are equal
    have h₅ : A = B := by
      apply Subtype.ext
      simp_all [QuadSols.mk.injEq]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₅
  exact h₃

@[sorryful]
lemma quadSolsIncl_injective (χ : ACCSystemQuad) :
    Function.Injective χ.quadSolsIncl := by
  intro A B h
  have h₁ : (χ.quadSolsIncl A : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) := h
  have h₂ : (A : χ.Charges) = (B : χ.Charges) := by
    have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl A : χ.Charges) = A.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    have h₄ : (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) = B.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    rw [h₃] at h₁
    rw [h₄] at h₁
    exact h₁
  -- Since A.val = B.val, we need to show that A = B as elements of QuadSols
  -- This requires checking that the quadratic conditions are also satisfied
  have h₃ : A = B := by
    -- Use the fact that A.val = B.val to show A = B
    have h₄ : A.val = B.val := h₂
    -- Since A and B are in QuadSols, their underlying charges are equal
    -- and their quadratic conditions are automatically satisfied
    -- because they are derived from the same charge
    apply Subtype.ext
    <;> simp_all [QuadSols.mk.injEq]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try linarith)
  exact h₃

  have h₂ : (A : χ.Charges) = (B : χ.Charges) := by

      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    have h₄ : (χ.quadSolsIncl B : χ.Charges) = B.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, linSolsIncl, ModuleHom.toFun_eq]
      <;> rfl
    rw [h₃] at h₁
    rw [h₄] at h₁
    exact h₁
  have h₃ : A.val = B.val := h₂
  -- Since A.val = B.val, we can use the fact that the inclusion from QuadSols to Charges is injective
  have h₄ : A = B := by
    cases A <;> cases B <;> simp_all [QuadSols.mk.injEq]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try linarith)
  exact h₄

refine' Function.Injective.comp h₁ _
    exact h₂
  exact h₃

have h₁ : Function.Injective χ.linSolsIncl := by
    intro S T h
    have h₂ : χ.linSolsIncl S = χ.linSolsIncl T := h
    have h₃ : S.val = T.val := by
      simp [linSolsIncl] at h₂ ⊢
      <;> aesop
    exact by
      cases S <;> cases T <;> simp_all [linSolsIncl]
      <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Function.Injective]
            <;>
            aesop
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            ext i
            simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]
            <;>
            aesop
          })
      <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Function.Injective]
            <;>
            aesop
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            ext i
            simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]
            <;>
            aesop
          })
  
  have h₂ : Function.Injective χ.quadSolsInclLinSols := by
    intro S T h
    have h₃ : χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S = χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T := h
    have h₄ : S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols := by
      simpa [quadSolsInclLinSols] using h₃
    have h₅ : S.val = T.val := by
      simp_all [LinSols.val_inj]
    have h₆ : ∀ i : Fin χ.numberQuadratic, (χ.quadraticACCs i) S.val = 0 := S.quadSol
    have h₇ : ∀ i : Fin χ.numberQuadratic, (χ.quadraticACCs i) T.val = 0 := T.quadSol
    have h₈ : S = T := by
      cases S
      cases T
      simp_all [QuadSols.mk.injEq]
      <;>
      (try {
        ext i
        <;> simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]
        <;> aesop
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Function.Injective]
        <;> aesop
      })
    exact h₈
  
  have h₃ : Function.Injective χ.quadSolsIncl := by
    intro S T h
    have h₄ : χ.quadSolsIncl S = χ.quadSolsIncl T := h
    have h₅ : χ.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S) = χ.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T) := by
      calc
        χ.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S) = χ.quadSolsIncl S := by
          simp [quadSolsIncl, mulActionHom_comp_apply]
          <;>
          aesop
        _ = χ.quadSolsIncl T := h₄
        _ = χ.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T) := by
          simp [quadSolsIncl, mulActionHom_comp_apply]
          <;>
          aesop
    have h₆ : χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S = χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T := by
      apply h₁
      exact h₅
    have h₇ : S = T := by
      apply h₂
      exact h₆
    exact h₇
  
  exact h₃

intro S T h
  have h₁ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) := h
  have h₂ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = S.val := by simp [quadSolsIncl, MulActionHom.comp_apply, χ.quadSolsInclLinSols.map_smul]
  have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) = T.val := by simp [quadSolsIncl, MulActionHom.comp_apply, χ.quadSolsInclLinSols.map_smul]
  have h₄ : S.val = T.val := by rw [h₂, h₃] at h₁; exact h₁
  have h₅ : S.toLinSols.val = T.toLinSols.val := by
    simp_all [QuadSols.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  have h₆ : S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols := by
    apply ACCSystemLinear.LinSols.ext
    <;> simp_all
  simp_all [QuadSols.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

intro S T h
  have h₁ : S.val = T.val := by
    -- Since `χ.quadSolsIncl S = S.val` and similarly for `T`, we get `S.val = T.val`
    have h₂ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) := h
    have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = S.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, MulActionHom.comp_apply, linSolsIncl, AccSystemLinear.toLinSols_val]
      <;> rfl
    have h₄ : (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) = T.val := by
      simp [quadSolsIncl, MulActionHom.comp_apply, linSolsIncl, AccSystemLinear.toLinSols_val]
      <;> rfl
    rw [h₃] at h₂
    rw [h₄] at h₂
    exact h₂
  -- Since `S.val = T.val`, by `QuadSols.ext`, we have `S = T`
  have h₂ : S = T := by
    apply QuadSols.ext
    exact h₁
  exact h₂

have h₁ : Function.Injective (χ.quadSolsIncl : χ.QuadSols → χ.Charges) := by
    intro S T h
    have h₂ : S.val = T.val := by
      have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) := by simpa using h
      have h₄ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = S.val := by
        simp [χ.quadSolsIncl, χ.linSolsIncl, MulActionHom.toFun_eq_coe]
        <;> rfl
      have h₅ : (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) = T.val := by
        simp [χ.quadSolsIncl, χ.linSolsIncl, MulActionHom.toFun_eq_coe]
        <;> rfl
      rw [h₄, h₅] at h₃
      exact h₃
    have h₃ : S.toLinSols.val = T.toLinSols.val := by
      simp_all [QuadSols.val, LinSols.val]
    have h₄ : S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols := by
      apply ACCSystemLinear.LinSols.ext
      <;> simp_all [QuadSols.val, LinSols.val]
    have h₅ : S = T := by
      apply Subtype.ext
      <;> simp_all [QuadSols.val, LinSols.val]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [ACCSystemLinear.LinSols.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.forall_fin_succ]) <;>
      (try aesop)
    exact h₅
  
  -- Since the inclusion map is injective, we can directly use this fact to conclude the proof.
  exact h₁

intro S T h
  have h₁ : (S : χ.Charges) = (T : χ.Charges) := by simpa using h
  have h₂ : S = T := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    exact h₁
  exact h₂

intro S T h
  have h₁ : S.val = T.val := by
    have h₂ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = (χ.quadSolsIncl T : χ.Charges) := h
    have h₃ : (χ.quadSolsIncl S : χ.Charges) = S.val := by

  have h₂ : S = T := by

      <;>

intro S T h
  have h₁ : χ.quadSolsIncl S = χ.quadSolsIncl T := h
  have h₂ : χ.1.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S) = χ.1.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T) := by
    calc
      χ.1.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S) = χ.quadSolsIncl S := by
        simp [quadSolsIncl, MulActionHom.comp_apply]
      _ = χ.quadSolsIncl T := h₁
      _ = χ.1.linSolsIncl (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T) := by
        simp [quadSolsIncl, MulActionHom.comp_apply]
  have h₃ : χ.quadSolsInclLinSols S = χ.quadSolsInclLinSols T := by
    have h₄ : Function.Injective (χ.1.linSolsIncl : χ.1.LinSols →ₗ[ℚ] χ.1.1.Charges) := χ.1.linSolsIncl_injective
    exact h₄ h₂
  have h₄ : S = T := by
    have h₅ : Function.Injective (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols : χ.QuadSols →[ℚ] χ.1.LinSols) := quadSolsInclLinSols_injective χ
    exact h₅ h₃
  exact h₄

end ACCSystemQuad

intro S T h
  have h₁ : χ.quadSolsIncl S = χ.quadSolsIncl T := h
  simp only [quadSolsIncl] at h₁
  have h₂ : S.toLinSols.val = T.toLinSols.val := by
    have h₃ := congr_arg (fun x => x.val) h₁
    simp [MulActionHom.toFun] at h₃ ⊢
    <;>
    (try simp_all [QuadSols, LinSols, ACCSystemQuad, ACCSystemLinear, ACCSystemCharges])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
    <;>
    (try omega)
  have h₃ : S = T := by
    have h₄ : S.toLinSols = T.toLinSols := by
      apply Subtype.ext
      <;> simp_all
      <;>
      (try simp_all [QuadSols, LinSols, ACCSystemQuad, ACCSystemLinear, ACCSystemCharges])
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
      <;>
      (try omega)
    have h₅ : S.val = T.val := by
      simp_all [QuadSols, LinSols, ACCSystemQuad, ACCSystemLinear, ACCSystemCharges]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
      <;>
      (try omega)
    exact Subtype.ext h₅
  exact h₃

have h₃ : Function.Injective χ.linSolsIncl := by
      -- Prove that the linear solutions inclusion is injective
      intro S T h
      -- Extract the equality of the underlying charges
      have h₄ : S.val = T.val := by
        simpa [linSolsIncl] using h
      -- Use the extensionality lemma for linear solutions
      exact LinSols.ext h₄
    -- Use the fact that the linear solutions inclusion is injective
    exact h₃
  
  have h₃ : Function.Injective ⇑(ACCSystemQuad.quadSolsIncl χ) := by
    have h₄ : Function.Injective (MulActionHom.comp χ.linSolsIncl.toMulActionHom χ.quadSolsInclLinSols) := by
      -- Use the fact that the composition of injective functions is injective
      have h₅ : Function.Injective χ.quadSolsInclLinSols := h₁
      have h₆ : Function.Injective ⇑χ.linSolsIncl := h₂
      -- Use the property of injective functions to prove the composition is injective
      have h₇ : Function.Injective (MulActionHom.comp χ.linSolsIncl.toMulActionHom χ.quadSolsInclLinSols) := by
        -- Use the fact that the composition of injective functions is injective
        have h₈ : Function.Injective χ.quadSolsInclLinSols := h₁
        have h₉ : Function.Injective ⇑χ.linSolsIncl := h₂
        -- Use the property of injective functions to prove the composition is injective
        apply Function.Injective.comp (χ.quadSolsInclLinSols) h₂
      exact h₇
    -- Use the fact that the composition of injective functions is injective
    simpa [quadSolsIncl] using h₄
  
  exact h₃

refine' fun S T h => _
  have h₁ : (S.val : χ.Charges) = (T.val : χ.Charges) := by
    simpa [χ.quadSolsIncl] using h
  have h₂ : S.val = T.val := by
    simpa using h₁
  have h₃ : S = T := by
    apply Subtype.ext
    <;> simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
  exact h₃
